list.name: Changes
list.comment: more updated annotations and exported updated disassembly. Added cdl export.

task.summary: Default task
Small todo change.
A few fixes.
Added a routine that gets the character widths for each font tile.
Added many changes and new script files.
refined the translation. working on vwf

Huge changes. Folded in some very old stuff to supplement the most recent breakthroughs. Fixed some script bugs. The text rendering is currently broken. Working on DMA code and the current rendering is broken. Rethinking the VWF buffer layout and rendering.
VWF is partially working without crashing. Control codes are not implemented. width map is very wrong, but I am optimistic.
Updated disassembly documentation. Changed Charley's name spelling. Changed Kurukuru to Spiral for obvious reasons. Updated various documents.
static rendering is working for VWF! Still work to do, but it's progress!
Committing some changes. Relly annoyed that the vwf patch is fully broken once more. Renamed the current version for archival and rolling back to 4.2 (last commit)

Add single-command build system to lm3.py Commands: font, script, vwf, build, extract via argparse CLI. - font: converts font_accented.png → font_1bppil.bin + font_1bpp.bin + widths.bin - script: patches font into ROM + inserts all translated text tables → lm3_scripted.sfc - vwf: applies vwf_patch.asm via asar to scripted ROM → lm3_en.sfc (fast iteration) - build: full pipeline in one command
Adds SCRIPT_TABLES constant, insert_table_into_rom() helper (in-place bytearray), insert_all_scripts(), build_font(), build_scripted(), build_vwf(), build(). Also adds pillow and chardet as tracked dependencies.
Big changes for script dumping and insertion. Still some bugs, but we'll figure it out.
Round-trip verification now passing for original data.
More fixes for round-trip verification. Identified interleaved data in between text sections related to menus. Going to need to trace and document this before relocation can be functional.
New text blocks have been identified for translation.

146 unit name translations (16-byte expanded) 36 unit class translations (12 bytes) 128 item translations (9 bytes) expansion plan for equipment/item names Added FIXED_TABLES config, insert_fixed_table(), insert_all_fixed(), extract configs for 3 new tables, fill-byte trimming fix, build pipeline integration Unit name expansion ASM patch: relocated table to bank $C4 (16 bytes/entry), redirect functions via JSL to expanded code with 4×ASL

Script intro patch. The org $80B923 directive was 2 bytes too early. $B923 is the BRA instruction at the end of copyUnitFirstName, not the start of copyUnitSurname. The actual surname function starts at $B925, which is what the game's JSR $B925 (at $80:B8FE) targets. After patching, $B925 contained the bank byte of the JSL target ($80), which the CPU interpreted as a BRA $04 — sliding into the NOPped-out shared loop and crashing. Changed org $80B923 → org $80B925, extended the first patch to cover the 2 extra BRA bytes, and adjusted NOP padding.
Fixed some english script files that were missing control codes.
Committing changes before we do some big edits to script files.
Some huge changes and improvements to the quality of the decoding/encoding logic. Now we have a validate-en that checks script files against the original dump to ensure control codes are respected.
Fixed a bunch of stuff, but now the rom wont load, so...
Implemented DTE. DTE uses control code FF Instead of old method. Needs testing. Worked toward extraction/insertion 1:1.
Added old DTE code for fun. Originally written by bongo, but one of the things that helped me learn assembly back in the day. Added separate metatable patches. Added pointer finder heuristics.
Now using AI to help quickly iterate fixes. Added IPC connection to the custom Mesen debugger. Fixed some issues with the mattle-msg script

quietly broke any table where the extractor emitted entries out of their original sub-entry order.
battle-menu.txt triggered it: the extractor placed entry :0 at file position 17 (because entry 0's data pointer sorts after entries 1-17 in the ROM's original layout). Every sub-entry in the battle region of meta-table entry 0 ended up off-by-one — sub 33's slot received battle-menu entry 0's "select unit" text, while the original sub 33 content (the shared "Are you sure?" YES/NO confirmation dialog) landed in sub 32's slot.
Symptom: scenario info screen (e.g. Fusion Temple) showed body text cut off after "You're done" with an empty YES/NO prompt box. The shared confirmation dialog is loaded from battle-menu sub 33 by the scenario info function at $01:EB0F, so every screen that pops a YES/NO confirmation saw wrong content once metatbl_battle.asm relocated entry 0 to $C5.
The fix parses `:(\d+)` from the header, stores entries keyed by that index, then emits encoded_entries in range(0, max_idx+1) order, filling any missing indices with empty stubs. Falls back to file order if a header has no :N component (preserves behavior for any non-conforming tables).
Diagnosis notes: - TextPtrDispatch and the 3-byte $C5 ptr table were both correct; verified by read-breakpoint on $228000-$228257 — hit inside TextPtrDispatch at $2E:AA7B with $14/$16 = $C5:$8000 resolving sub 33 to a valid target. - Phase 1 text buffer at WRAM $0400 contained the full resolved text including the tail prompt, but phase 2 rendered only up to "You're done" because sub 33's bytes were unrelated data for the renderer to process. - Byte-level comparison of sub 33 data at $C5:$8E42 vs. original $02:$BE16 revealed completely different content; the original sub 33 signature `FF 15 1A FF 80 04 FF FC 82 00` was found in the new build at $C5:$8DCD (sub 32's slot), confirming the off-by-one shift. - scenario-desc, script, quiz-text, etc. were unaffected because their extractions happened to be in natural index order already.
Minor unrelated observation (not fixed here): battle-menu entry 17 contains `[c]` which isn't defined in eng.tbl, so the encoder emits literal ASCII `5B 63 5D` instead of the original single byte `$40`. Does not affect the metatbl redirect path.
Improved/fixed translations.
committing inconsistent state as a backup
removed a bunch of asm patches now that the FFC0 control code is understood. it's like a built in relocation code. I can relocate text willy-nilly anytime.
current build success for script and scene desc
encoding fixup
fixed encoding for event-text script
Translation formatting fixes.
committing a ton of event script changes, etc
formatting fixes
More changes and fixes.
Aded new master rom hacking database for ai to reference. Added recent changes.
Added windowed script file caching. some script fixes.
Removed garbage from script_ext and reformatted to use the window format.
more changes to script. need debugged
updated annotations and exported updated disassembly
more updated annotations and exported updated disassembly. Added cdl export.

MESSAGE.value: Fixed some english script files that were missing control codes.
Committing changes before we do some big edits to script files.
Some huge changes and improvements to the quality of the decoding/encoding logic. Now we have a validate-en that checks script files against the original dump to ensure control codes are respected.
Fixed a bunch of stuff, but now the rom wont load, so...
Implemented DTE. DTE uses control code FF Instead of old method. Needs testing. Worked toward extraction/insertion 1:1.
Added old DTE code for fun. Originally written by bongo, but one of the things that helped me learn assembly back in the day. Added separate metatable patches. Added pointer finder heuristics.
Now using AI to help quickly iterate fixes. Added IPC connection to the custom Mesen debugger. Fixed some issues with the mattle-msg script

quietly broke any table where the extractor emitted entries out of their original sub-entry order.
battle-menu.txt triggered it: the extractor placed entry :0 at file position 17 (because entry 0's data pointer sorts after entries 1-17 in the ROM's original layout). Every sub-entry in the battle region of meta-table entry 0 ended up off-by-one — sub 33's slot received battle-menu entry 0's "select unit" text, while the original sub 33 content (the shared "Are you sure?" YES/NO confirmation dialog) landed in sub 32's slot.
Symptom: scenario info screen (e.g. Fusion Temple) showed body text cut off after "You're done" with an empty YES/NO prompt box. The shared confirmation dialog is loaded from battle-menu sub 33 by the scenario info function at $01:EB0F, so every screen that pops a YES/NO confirmation saw wrong content once metatbl_battle.asm relocated entry 0 to $C5.
The fix parses `:(\d+)` from the header, stores entries keyed by that index, then emits encoded_entries in range(0, max_idx+1) order, filling any missing indices with empty stubs. Falls back to file order if a header has no :N component (preserves behavior for any non-conforming tables).
Diagnosis notes: - TextPtrDispatch and the 3-byte $C5 ptr table were both correct; verified by read-breakpoint on $228000-$228257 — hit inside TextPtrDispatch at $2E:AA7B with $14/$16 = $C5:$8000 resolving sub 33 to a valid target. - Phase 1 text buffer at WRAM $0400 contained the full resolved text including the tail prompt, but phase 2 rendered only up to "You're done" because sub 33's bytes were unrelated data for the renderer to process. - Byte-level comparison of sub 33 data at $C5:$8E42 vs. original $02:$BE16 revealed completely different content; the original sub 33 signature `FF 15 1A FF 80 04 FF FC 82 00` was found in the new build at $C5:$8DCD (sub 32's slot), confirming the off-by-one shift. - scenario-desc, script, quiz-text, etc. were unaffected because their extractions happened to be in natural index order already.
Minor unrelated observation (not fixed here): battle-menu entry 17 contains `[c]` which isn't defined in eng.tbl, so the encoder emits literal ASCII `5B 63 5D` instead of the original single byte `$40`. Does not affect the metatbl redirect path.
Improved/fixed translations.
committing inconsistent state as a backup
removed a bunch of asm patches now that the FFC0 control code is understood. it's like a built in relocation code. I can relocate text willy-nilly anytime.
current build success for script and scene desc
encoding fixup
fixed encoding for event-text script
Translation formatting fixes.
committing a ton of event script changes, etc
formatting fixes
More changes and fixes.
Aded new master rom hacking database for ai to reference. Added recent changes.
Added windowed script file caching. some script fixes.
Removed garbage from script_ext and reformatted to use the window format.
more changes to script. need debugged
updated annotations and exported updated disassembly
more updated annotations and exported updated disassembly. Added cdl export.

option.name: LAST_COMMIT_MESSAGE
option.value: more updated annotations and exported updated disassembly. Added cdl export.
